tavily.raw: {"query":"2024 Nobel Prize Chemistry laureates official institutional affiliations at time of announcement","follow_up_questions":null,"answer":null,"images":[],"results":[{"url":"https://www.nobelprize.org/prizes/chemistry/2024/press-release","title":"Press release: The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024","content":"# Nobel Prize. The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences has decided to award the Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024. University of Washington, Seattle, WA, USA. Professor at University of Washington, Seattle, WA, USA and Investigator, Howard Hughes Medical Institute, USA. **Prize amount**: 11 million Swedish kronor, with one half to David Baker and the other half jointly to Demis Hassabis and John Jumper. The Nobel Prize in Chemistry 2024 is about pro­teins, life’s ingenious chemical tools. Demis Hassabis and John Jumper have developed an AI model to solve a 50-year-old problem: predicting proteins’ complex structures. The other is about fulfilling a 50-year-old dream: predicting protein structures from their amino acid sequences. In 2003, **David Baker** succeeded in using these blocks to design a new protein that was unlike any other protein. In proteins, amino acids are linked together in long strings that fold up to make a three-dimensional structure, which is decisive for the protein’s function. and the other half jointly to.","score":0.818055,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://grokipedia.com/page/List_of_Nobel_laureates_by_university_affiliation","title":"List of Nobel laureates by university affiliation — Grokipedia","content":"This article catalogs the affiliations of Nobel Prize laureates with universities and other academic or research institutions, primarily recording the institution they were associated with at the time of the award announcement.](#ref-1) These affiliations span all six Nobel Prize categories—Physics, Chemistry, Physiology or Medicine, Literature, Peace, and Economic Sciences—established since 1901, and reflect the pivotal role of higher education and research organizations in advancing human knowledge and societal progress.](#ref-2)](#ref-1) As of 2025, the Nobel Prizes have recognized 1,026 laureates (individuals and organizations) through 633 awards, with a significant portion linked to institutional affiliations that underscore collaborative scientific and intellectual achievements.](#ref-2) The official compilation encompasses 233 distinct institutions, predominantly universities, revealing a concentration of laureates at elite academic centers.](#ref-1) **Harvard University** leads with 29 affiliated laureates, followed by the **Massachusetts Institute of Technology** with 25, the **California Institute of Technology** with 20, **Columbia University** with 18, and the **University of California, Berkeley** with 16.](#ref-1) This distribution highlights the dominance of U.S.-based institutions, which account for the majority of affiliations, while also including notable contributions from European and other global centers such as the University of Chicago (14 laureates) and Stanford University (15).](#ref-1) The list serves as a metric for institutional impact, though it focuses on affiliations at the award moment and does not account for lifetime or educational connections.](#ref-1).","score":0.784336,"raw_content":null},{"url":"https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=bPjB9NRu8Jc","title":"Announcement of the 2024 Nobel Prize in Chemistry","content":"The Royal
